title: Star Tours: The Adventures Continue
content:
{{Top|rwm|italics=1}} {{Movie |image=[[File:StarTours_II_poster.jpg]] |title=''Star Tours: The Adventures Continue'' |director= |producer=[[Tom Fitzgerald]]{{Disney|domain=go.com|url=disneyfans/insider/article/20110510|text=Countdown to an All-New Star Tours|date=2011-05-10}} |production company= |writer=[[Steven Spiegel]]{{DPB|url=2010/10/on-the-set-of-star-tours-with-c-3po|text=On the Set of Star Tours with C-3PO|nolive=1}} |starring=*[[Anthony Daniels]] *[[Allison Janney]]{{DPB|url=2010/09/these-are-the-droids-youre-looking-for|text=These ARE the Droids You're Looking For!}} *[[James Earl Jones]] *[[Frank Oz]] *[[John Boyega]] {{C|2015 addition}} *[[Oscar Isaac]] {{C|2017 addition}} *[[Billy Dee Williams]] {{C|2019 addition}} |music=*[[Michael Giacchino]]{{WebCite|url=http://www.laughingplace.com/Latest-ID-75247.asp|text=Composer Michael Giacchino to Score Star Tours II: The Adventures Continue?|date=2010-08-17|archivedate=20120920205744}} *[[John Williams]] |release date=*[[May 20]], [[2011]] {{C|Disney's Hollywood Studios}} *[[June 3]], 2011 {{C|Disneyland}} *[[May 7]], [[2013]] {{C|Tokyo Disneyland}} *[[March 18]], [[2017]] {{C|Disneyland Paris}} |runtime=4 min. 30 sec. |budget= |language= |timeline=c. [[1 BBY]]{{SW|url=news/convenient-daily-departures-the-history-of-star-tours|text=Convenient Daily Departures: The History of ''Star Tours''|int=Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours|nolive=1}} |series= |preceded by=[[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] |followed by= }} {{Quote|We're going to do things with ''Star Tours'' that have never been done in any theme park attraction, at any theme park anywhere.|[[Wikipedia:Walt Disney Parks and Resorts|Walt Disney Parks and Resorts]] Chairman [[Wikipedia:Jay Rasulo|Jay Rasulo]]|{{SWArchive|url=fans/events/news20090912/index.html|text=A Re-Imagined Star Tours to Blast Off in California and Florida in 2011}}}} '''''Star Tours: The Adventures Continue''''' is a [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] [[Wikipedia:Simulator ride|simulator ride]] [[Wikipedia:Amusement park|theme park]] attraction located in [[Disneyland]] at [[Wikipedia:Disneyland Resort|Disneyland Resort]] in [[Wikipedia:California|California]], [[Disney's Hollywood Studios]] at [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney World Resort|Walt Disney World]] in [[Wikipedia:Florida|Florida]], [[Tokyo Disneyland]] at [[Wikipedia:Tokyo Disney Resort|Tokyo Disney Resort]] in [[Wikipedia:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[Wikipedia:Japan|Japan]] and [[Disneyland Paris|Disneyland Park]] at [[Wikipedia:Disneyland Paris|Disneyland Paris]] in [[Wikipedia:Paris|Paris]], [[Wikipedia:France|France]]. It officially opened in Florida on [[May 20]], [[2011]],{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/latestnews/story/Star_Tours_Returns_Starting_May_20th_136170.asp|text=Star Tours Returns Starting May 20th|author=Geller, Eric|date=2011-01-18|archivedate=20190729153331}} in California on [[June 3]], 2011,{{DPB|url=2011/02/from-under-the-sea-to-galaxies-far-far-away-opening-dates-are-set-for-a-soundsational-summer-at-disneyland-resort|text=From Under the Sea to Galaxies Far, Far Away…Opening Dates Are Set For A Soundsational Summer at Disneyland Resort}} in Tokyo on [[May 7]], [[2013]]{{EndorExpress|url=parks/disneyparks/tokyo-disneyland/2013/05/konnichiwa-star-tours|text=Konnichiwa, Star Tours!}} and in Paris on [[March 18]], [[2017]].{{WebCite|url=https://radiodisneyclub.fr/star-tours-2-disneyland-paris|text=Star Tours : The Adventure Continues à Disneyland Paris pour les 25 ans|author=Gauthier, Kevin|date=2014-10-07|language=French|archivedate=20190608192814}} Early promotional materials referred to the ride as '''''Star Tours II''''', while the alternate name '''''Star Tours 3-D''''' was used when the ride first opened.{{Facebook|username=StarTours|url=StarTours/photos/a.166585873397060/326257560763223|name=Star Tours (Orlando, FL)|date=March 29, 2012|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/8ykRL}} The attraction replaced the original [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] attraction and serves as its prequel, taking place between [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|Episodes III]] and [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|IV]] and tying both trilogies together. Additional elements from [[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|Episode VII]] were added in late 2015 to promote the new film, while scenes depicting the planets [[Crait]] and [[Batuu]] from [[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|Episode VIII]] and [[Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge|''Star Wars'': Galaxy's Edge]] respectively were added on [[November 17]], [[2017]]. Additions from [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|Episode IX]] featuring the moon of [[Kef Bir]] and [[Exegol]] debuted on [[December 20]], [[2019]].{{DPB|url=2019/10/here-we-go-again-exciting-updates-to-star-tours-the-adventures-continue|text='Here We Go Again!' Exciting Updates to Star Tours — The Adventures Continue}} At the [[Celebration Europe (2023)|''Star Wars'' Celebration Europe 2023]], it was announced that more destinations would be added to the rides in California, Florida and Paris in [[2024]],{{DPB|url=2023/04/star-tours-sets-course-for-new-adventures-in-2024|text=Star Tours Sets Course for New Adventures in 2024}} with Disneyland's debut of the those scenes taking place during the [[Season of the Force]] event from [[April 5]]th to [[June 2]]nd.{{SW|url=news/season-of-the-force|text=Season of the Force, New Star Tours Adventures, and ''Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge'' Surprises Coming to Disneyland Park}} Some footage from the initial version of the ride appeared years later as part of a flight simulator in the [[Star Wars: Millennium Falcon (attraction)|''Star Wars'': Millennium Falcon]] themed area of the ''Disney Dream'' cruise ship.{{YouTube|video=MFmiGbztIxo|text=Star Wars: Millennium Falcon - Disney Dream - Disney Cruise Line|channel=UC7SjQGMropNWcVKwtwVQkbg|name=The DIS}} ==Opening crawl== This crawl was shown at the attraction's grand opening ceremony.{{DPB|url=2011/05/live-webcast-star-tours-the-adventures-continue-at-disneys-hollywood-studios|text=Webcast: 'Star Tours – The Adventures Continue' at Disney's Hollywood Studios}}{{InsiderCite|127|The Incredible Journey}} {{OpeningCrawl |name=Episode III.V
Star Tours |text=It has been a long time since the end of the Clone Wars, and the evil Sith Lord Darth Vader continues to tighten his grip on the Empire as the galaxy moves closer to the brink of a great civil war. A new intergalactic spaceline, Star Tours, seeks to preserve unrestricted intergalactic travel in this age of tyranny. Freedom fighter Captain Raymus Antilles has assigned two droids, See-Threepio and Artoo-Detoo, to help launch the spaceline, fueling Imperial suspicion that Star Tours is part of the Rebel Alliance. Star Tours is about to open its first intergalactic space terminal in the Earth System as rumors of a fearsome weapon of mass destruction dash all hope for peace and freedom in the galaxy…}} ==Plot summary== [[File:Star_Tours_2_cabin.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ride's passenger cabin]] Unlike the original ''Star Tours'', the ride has twenty-one (originally eleven) segments that are randomly chosen during each viewing. In all, the various options resulted in 54 possible combinations.{{WebCite|url=http://theforce.net/latestnews/story/Star_Tours_The_Adventures_Continue_Review_138213.asp|text=''Star Tours: The Adventures Continue'' Review|author=Wyman, Chris|date=2011-05-16|archivedate=20190730105318}}{{WebCite|url=http://www.wdwmagic.com/attractions/star-tours-3d/news/14may2011-soft-opening-preview-report---star-tours-the-adventures-continue.htm|text=Soft opening preview report - Star Tours The Adventures Continue|date=2011-05-14|archivedate=20160819215756}} Two new segments added in 2015 to promote ''The Force Awakens'' were locked into place for the duration of the Season of the Force event, after which the randomization was re-enabled. Additionally, variations of existing segments created in 2011 were activated for the first time,{{Disney|subdomain=blogs|url=insider/2016/01/04/why-star-tours-is-now-better-than-ever|text=Why Star Tours Is Now Better Than Ever}} increasing variability further during the lock. The 2017 addition of material to promote ''The Last Jedi'' brought in more [[sequel trilogy]] content to create a full experience focused on the era as a result of criticism of the [[Jakku]] segment mismatching the rest of the attraction. The 2019 addition of ''The Rise of Skywalker'' material, initially presented as a locked experience, allowed the sequel trilogy to share more of the original unpredictability by providing two additional planets to shuffle through when the lock was removed.{{WebCite|url=https://www.ocregister.com/2019/12/22/rise-of-skywalker-star-tours-update-pays-tribute-to-original-1987-disneyland-attraction|work=The Orange County Register|archivedate=20230607213651|text=‘Rise of Skywalker’ Star Tours update pays tribute to original 1987 Disneyland attraction}} 2024 saw the addition of [[Seatos]] as a second planet option and additional hologram messages from [[Cassian Jeron Andor|Cassian Andor]], [[Ahsoka Tano]] with [[Huyang]] and [[C1-10P|Chopper]], and [[Din Djarin]] with [[Din Grogu|Grogu]].{{DPB|url=2024/03/ahsoka-andor-and-the-mandalorian-inspire-new-star-tours-adventures|text=Ahsoka, Andor and The Mandalorian Inspire New ''Star Tours'' Adventures}} While Ahsoka and Cassian specifically reference the Rebellion, Din's message is more ambiguous. Scenes based on the [[sequel trilogy]] and new [[canon]] television series are considered non-canon since the original ride is part of the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends continuity]]. ===Queue and boarding=== In one feature exclusive to the version of the ride at Tokyo Disneyland, guests first enter a room with a large video billboard advertising various travel destinations and revealing other details of the story, such as identifying the Star Tours terminal as only one part of [[Spaceport THX1138]].{{YouTube|video=VP1Ms45_fZ8|text=TDL : New STAR TOURS【Space Port 1138】|channel=@couperin9|name=couperin9}} [[File:Star_Tours_2_queue_StarSpeeder_1000.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ride queue]]In every version of the ride, guests then enter a hangar where [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] are busy repairing a [[StarSpeeder 1000]] while a large screen displays commercials (some hosted by [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Aly San San/Legends|Aly San San]]), messages, flight lists and exterior views of a space station that presumably the waiting passengers are now inside, just like in the original attraction. Guests pass by security droid [[G2-9T/Legends|G2-9T]] X-raying luggage, where a monitor displays various items inside the luggage (including many ''Star Wars'' and Disney references). Guests also pass by droid customs, where [[RX-24/Legends|RX-24]] is malfunctioning with a sticker labeled "Defective: return to factory." After passing another droid screening passengers, [[G2-4T]], guests reach the boarding area and watch video screens that display the outside of the StarSpeeder maintenance area where C-3PO speaks with the ship's pilot droid, [[AC-38/Legends|AC-38]], also known as Ace. C-3PO needs to sit in the pilot's seat to repair the binary motivator. As Ace leaves to inspect the outside of the ship, C-3PO sits on the seat to begin the maintenance. But before he can do so, the autopilot unexpectedly engages. The cockpit shield lowers and seals, thus trapping C-3PO inside. Meanwhile, Ace is inspecting the outside of the ship but then gets called away by someone else from the area. Guests then watch a safety video hosted by Aly San San. Ace does not return before the ship is elevated to the passenger boarding level, leaving C-3PO and R2-D2 on board the flight with passengers. [[Star Tours Flight 1401]] is then cleared for take off despite C-3PO's protests, and R2-D2 takes control of the vessel. ===Spaceport escape=== {{Main|Raid at Spaceport THX1138}} This segment includes one of six scenes. Sequel-trilogy-era variants are non-canon. *As the ship prepares to leave the hangar, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] flies into view on a [[Hover Platform]], flanked by [[Jumptrooper/Legends|Sky troopers]] and [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to prevent the ship from leaving. A video screen identifies one of the passengers as a [[Unidentified Rebel spy|Rebel spy]]. Vader uses [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to stop the ship until R2-D2 fires the [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], and the StarSpeeder exits the hangar backwards and is chased by [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]]. The ship then evades [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and escapes into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. '''Alternate version:''' In another version of the scene, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] joins Vader and the Sky troopers. *While the StarSpeeder is docked near the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], a droid scans the ship and one of the passengers is identified as a Rebel spy, while outside Solo gets into a firefight with stormtroopers. The StarSpeeder then follows the ''Falcon'' as it flees the hangar and flies past Star Destroyers (causing severe damage to one's bridge) before entering hyperspace. *'''Non-canon:''' In a scene patterned after the original Darth Vader scene, [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]] and [[First Order stormtrooper]]s arrive, and Ren uses the Force to prevent the ship from leaving. After he identifies a spy and "traitor to the [[First Order]]," R2-D2 opens fire, and the ship flees as in the earlier version. (Added in 2017 to promote ''The Last Jedi''.) *'''Non-canon:''' In a scene similar to the probe droid scene featuring Han Solo and the ''Millennium Falcon'', First Order stormtroopers join the droid while Rey and BB-8 replace Solo as they flee in the ''Falcon''. (Added in 2017 to promote ''The Last Jedi''.) *'''Non-canon:''' In another variation of the probe droid scene, it is Ahsoka Tano's [[T-6 1974]] ship that flees the stormtroopers with the StarSpeeder following close behind. (Added in 2024 to promote the ''Ahsoka'' television series.) ===Planet 1=== The ship exits hyperspace above one of five planets. Sequel-trilogy-era variants are non-canon. *[[Battle of Hoth (1 BBY)]]: The ship reaches the surface of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] and follows [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeders]] in an attack run on an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]]. The ship is struck by walker fire, hits the ground, and slides down an icy mountain before flying back into space. *[[Skirmish on Kashyyyk (Galactic Civil War)|Skirmish on Kashyyyk]]: On [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the ship is caught in a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] chase through the forest with some [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]]. [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] is thrown from his bike and hits the ship's windshield. The speeder then leaves the forest and heads for space. *The ship arrives at [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and drops through the atmosphere into the middle of the [[Boonta_Eve_Classic/Legends#Boonta_Eve_Classic_(1_BBY)|Boonta Eve Classic]] [[Podracing/Legends|pod race]] of 1 BBY. After getting stuck to a podracer and having a wrench thrown at it by [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]], the speeder crosses the finish line and the podracer attached to it crashes before the speeder heads back into space. *'''Non-canon:''' The ship arrives on Jakku in the midst of a chase between the ''Millennium Falcon'' and a squad of [[First Order]] [[TIE/fo space superiority fighter|TIE fighters]] through the wreckage. [[Finn]] (or [[Rey Skywalker|Rey]] in an alternate version of the scene) appears on the screen asking what Star Tours is doing here and tells them to stay low. Diving into a wrecked Star Destroyer and avoiding debris, the ship stumbles into a group of scavengers, who steal the Star Tours seal. The ship then leaves before more damage can be done and heads for space. (Added in 2015 to promote ''The Force Awakens''.) *'''Non-canon:''' The ship drops from hyperspace flying past [[Endor]], which 3PO comments on, and lands in the oceans of the moon of [[Kef Bir]] near the ruins of the [[DS-2 Death Star II Mobile Battle Station]]. When ocean waves send the StarSpeeder inside these remnants of the Battle of Endor, the ship encounters a pack of [[Dianoga]] before R2 gets the ship able to take off and leave the moon. (Added in 2019 to promote ''The Rise of Skywalker''.) ===Hologram message=== Once back in space, the StarSpeeder receives a holographic message from one of ten different characters. Scenes based on the Sequel trilogy and new Canon television series are considered non-canon. *'''Legends''' **[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]], or [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Princess Leia]] explains that the passengers must help get the Rebel on board safely to a rendezvous point, and then sends the coordinates to R2-D2. *'''Non-canon''' **[[Ahsoka Tano]] (with her ship, [[Huyang]], and [[C1-10P|Chopper]] behind her) greets R2 and 3PO and explains that they must help get the Rebel on board safely to a rendezvous point she is sending them. (Added in 2024 to promote the ''Ahsoka'' series.) **A hooded figure reveals himself to be [[Cassian Jeron Andor|Cassian Andor]] and says he has an urgent mission for the Rebellion. He tells the ship to head to the coordinates he is sending. (Added in 2024 to promote the ''Andor'' series.) **[[Din Djarin]], accompanied by [[Din Grogu|Grogu]], tells the passengers that they can no longer stand in the shadows and asks them to join an unspecified battle while Grogu uses the Force to play with a frog before swallowing it. (Added in 2024 to promote ''The Mandalorian''.) **[[BB-8]] asks for help with a secret mission to deliver vital information. 3PO refuses, but R2 accepts and makes a hyperspace jump. (Added in 2015 to promote ''The Force Awakens''.) **[[Maz Kanata]], in the middle of a combat situation, asks the crew to join the Resistance and warns of the dark side's shadow spreading across the galaxy. (Added in 2017 to promote ''The Last Jedi''.) **[[Poe Dameron]], asking for help, is surprised at seeing R2 and 3PO before getting right to business and sending them the coordinates for their next destination. (Added in 2017 to promote ''The Last Jedi''.) **[[Landonis Balthazar Calrissian|Lando Calrissian]], happy to see R2 and 3PO, explains that the First Order is rallying its forces and the Resistance needs their help. He tells them to rendezvous at the coordinates he is transmitting. (Added in 2019 to promote ''The Rise of Skywalker''.) ===Planet 2=== After jumping through hyperspace, the ship reaches one of six destinations. The sequel-trilogy-era scenes are non-canon. *[[Incident above Coruscant]]: The ship ends up in the middle of a battle high above [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and is attacked by [[Pistoeka sabotage droid/Legends|Buzz droids]]. R2 takes care of one droid, while the others are knocked off in the battle. The ship then flies through the city traffic below before reaching a landing platform, where a number of parked StarSpeeders can be found. An alternate option added later has the ''Millennium Falcon'' and Chewbacca appear on the platform instead. *[[Skirmish over Naboo (Galactic Civil War)|Skirmish over Naboo]]: The StarSpeeder reaches [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in the middle of a space battle. The ship then follows a group of [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighters]] to the surface, and eventually lands in the ocean near a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] city, either dodging or crashing into [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] upon arrival. After traveling through the planet core and encountering dangerous sea creatures, the ship launches back to the surface and crash-lands in a Rebel hangar, where it crashes into an N-1. The N-1's tail ends up inside the craft, smashing into the viewport, as a [[DUM-series pit droid/Legends|pit droid]] angrily shouts at Threepio. Alternatively, the StarSpeeder only breaks the tail of the N-1 off, angering the pit droid working on it, resulting in him throwing paint onto the viewport. *[[Skirmish at Geonosis]]: In the [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]], the StarSpeeder is chased by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]. The ship escapes but then encounters the incomplete first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] while pursued by Vader and his [[TIE pilot]]s. The ship escapes by flying right through the unfinished Death Star, but are then met again by Boba, who shoots a [[Seismic charge/Legends|seismic charge]] at the ship. The StarSpeeder shoots it back, evading him and then heading into hyperspace to meet up with the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel fleet]] and land in the [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']]{{'s}} hangar bay. *'''Non-canon:''' [[Battle of Crait]]: The StarSpeeder arrives on Crait in the middle of the battle between the [[First Order]] and the [[Resistance]]. After escaping TIE Fighters by going through a cave, the StarSpeeder re-emerges in the midst of a battle with a group of [[All Terrain MegaCaliber Six]]es and after taking down a few, the ship escapes to hyperspace. (Added it 2017 to promote ''The Last Jedi''.) *'''Non-canon:''' [[Battle of Exegol]]: The StarSpeeder arrives in the skies over Exegol, where the Resistance is engaging the [[Sith Eternal military|Sith Eternal forces]]. As the ship dodges shots and debris in the chaotic battle, a frightened 3PO tells the audience he was happy to have them as passengers, assuming none of them will survive. After knocking a broken [[TIE/dg starfighter|Sith TIE fighter]] off the ship's viewport, the StarSpeeder helps take out a [[Xyston-class Star Destroyer|''Xyston''-class Star Destroyer]] and then jumps back into hyperspace. (Added in 2019 to promote ''The Rise of Skywalker''.) *'''Non-canon''': [[Incident on Seatos]]: Arriving in the skies of [[Seatos]], the StarSpeeder finds themselves in the middle of a pod of [[Purrgil]] while the [[T-6 1974]] is engaged in battle with two [[RP82 Fiend fighter]]s, with [[Ahsoka Tano|Ahsoka]] asking for assistance. Weaving through the tentacles and avoiding being swallowed by one of the Purrgils, the StarSpeeder is able to take out one of the fighters and R2 has the ship spin in triumph to imitate Ahsoka's ship. Before the ship jumps away, Ahsoka wishes us safe travels and remarks that it was good to see R2 again. (Added in 2024 to promote ''Ahsoka''.) *'''Non-canon:''' If visiting sequel trilogy locations and Seatos, one of two additional ending scenes occurs. In one version, the ship escapes to [[Batuu]], where C-3PO muses that [[Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge|their adventure is just beginning]]. Alternatively, the StarSpeeder returns to [[Spaceport THX1138]]. After dodging several outgoing ships, we land on a lower level, frightening a group of pit droids and catching the ire of [[AC-38/Legends|AC-38]], who approaches the StarSpeeder while demanding an explanation before the viewport shield closes. ==Development== [[File:StarTours2_bg.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A scene from the re-vamped Star Tours]] After years of rumors and speculation of an updated version of ''Star Tours'', it was confirmed in April 2005, at [[Celebration III]], where creator [[George Lucas]] announced that a ''Star Tours II'' was in production.{{SWArchive|url=community/event/celebration/f20050419/indexp17.html|text=starwars.com at Celebration III - Thank the Maker: George Lucas}} It was claimed at the 2009 D23 Expo, the official Disney fan convention, that there would not be a second ride introduced, but that instead the existing ride would be updated and improved. The original rides closed in [[2010]] ([[July 27]] in California and [[September 8]] in Florida) to allow Disney engineers to begin the updates and renovations. This new iteration of ''Star Tours'' re-opened on [[May 20]], [[2011]] at Disney's Hollywood Studios in Florida, and [[June 3]], 2011 at Disneyland in California. The new ride is in 3-D, and features the [[StarSpeeder 1000]],{{Blogspot|subdomain=disneyandmore|url=2009/09/new-high-res-files-of-dumbo-little.html|name=Disney and More blog|text=New high-res pictures of Dumbo, Little Mermaid and Star Tours 2 attractions}} bursting into the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] pod race. In a high-resolution image of the scene provided to various media outlets, the 1000 designation on the speeder could be seen. It was also confirmed that the new ride would take passengers to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] among other destinations, and there were rumors that the [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] flight would be reworked as well so that passengers would actually ''go'' to Endor this time. A new queue video "commercial" for the attraction was released in August 2010 at [[Celebration V]] which showed the StarSpeeder traveling to [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], Endor and [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], though it was not confirmed that these would be destinations within the ride.{{SWArchive|url=fans/events/celebration_v_startourspromo/index.html|text=New Star Tours "Commercial"}} In April, the final destination list was released, adding [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] with the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]] under construction, [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], and [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] joining the already announced Tatooine and Coruscant.{{DPB|url=2011/04/star-tours-where-in-the-galaxy-are-we-going|text=Star Tours: Where in the Galaxy Are We Going?}} Due to timeline concerns by the Imagineers, they changed the plan for the Hoth sequence from featuring Imperial walkers to focusing on [[Wampa/Legends|wampas]] and [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntauns]]. However, when the plans for this version of the sequence were presented to George Lucas, he insisted on using the walkers and offered a continuity explanation that the Rebels and the Empire had a skirmish there before, and the reason the Rebels went back to establish Echo Base was that they believed the Empire would never suspect they would return there.{{WebCite|url=http://travel.usatoday.com/alliance/destinations/mouseplanet/post/2012/05/Taking-a-Trip-on-Star-Tours/685697/1|text=Taking a Trip on Star Tours|author=Korkis, Jim|date=2012-05-02|archivedate=20120627091717}} In September, the official Disney Parks Blog revealed two droids from the new attraction: a pilot droid from the AC series named [[AC-38/Legends|Ace]] (a predecessor to [[RX-24/Legends|RX-24]] from the original) and a "spokesbot" named [[Aly San San/Legends|Aly San San]], voiced by actress [[Allison Janney]]. The blog also revealed that the ride's story takes place between [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|Episode III]] and [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Episode IV]] ([[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]). The Destination D23 event that announced these new droids also revealed that there would be 54 different experiences of the attraction.{{WebCite|url=http://micechat.com/forums/d23-disney-twenty-three/144045-destination-d-live-coverage-9.html|text=Destination D LIVE Coverage|date=2010-09-25|archivedate=20140829161644}} In October, it was revealed that, although ''Star Tours'' uses AC Droid pilots, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] would pilot the StarSpeeder 1000 in the attraction, through some chaotic mistake. The article also revealed that RX-24 would return, due to his popularity with thousands of fans.{{DPB|url=2010/10/whos-flying-this-thing|text=Who's Flying This Thing?!}} The score of the ride was composed by Oscar winner [[Michael Giacchino]], who, according to some reports, was a big fan of the original attraction.{{WebCite|url=https://trekmovie.com/2010/08/20/star-trek-composer-michael-giacchino-scoring-new-star-wars-ride|text=Star Trek Composer Michael Giacchino Scoring New Star Wars Ride|author=Pascale, Anthony|date=2010-08-20|archivedate=20170919161411}} In January, it was announced that ''Star Tours: The Adventures Continue'' would open on [[May 20]], 2011 at Disney's Hollywood Studios in Florida. In February, it was announced that the ride would open on [[June 3]], 2011 at Disneyland in California. That same month, the Disney Parks Blog revealed that ride passengers would encounter [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and [[Jumptrooper/Legends|Imperial jumptroopers]].{{DPB|url=2011/02/the-stars-of-star-tours|text=The 'Stars' of Star Tours}} A May 2011 interview with Tom Fitzgerald revealed that [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] would also appear, and that [[James Earl Jones]] and [[Frank Oz]] would reprise their roles as Vader and [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. The interview also revealed that the StarSpeeders would travel underwater and slide on ice. On May 14, almost a week before its official opening, early previews of the ride began at Disney Hollywood Studios in Florida.{{WebCite|url=https://www.wdwmagic.com/attractions/star-tours-3d/news/14may2011-soft-opening-preview-report---star-tours-the-adventures-continue.htm|work=WDWMagic.com|archivedate=20240528170447|text=Soft opening preview report - Star Tours The Adventures Continue}} [[File:Star_Tours_TAC-Tokyo.jpg|250px|thumb|''Star Tours: The Adventures Continue'' entrance at Tokyo Disneyland]] Similar to the original ''Star Tours'', the ride varied depending on the theme park location. In November 2011, it was announced that a new ride would open at Tokyo Disneyland in Spring of 2013. Tokyo Disneyland featured most of the characters and aspects of the original American version of the rides at Disneyland and Disney's Hollywood Studios. However, it also featured some additional content, such as an in-depth explanation of the spaceport shown in the queue area and rides, including the name of the spaceport in question (Spaceport THX1138). It also showed additional info about the location advertisements, as well as additional characters and droid models. It also featured three language settings: [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]], [[High Galactic/Legends|English]], and Japanese. At the end of the ride, three droids—[[F-25-OJSx]], [[F-24-AMTx]], and [[HHG-RX]]—were in poses mirroring that of the [[W:c:Disney:Hitchhiking Ghosts|Hitchhiking Ghosts]] from the Disney ride ''Haunted Mansion''.{{DPB|url=2013/05/star-tours-the-adventures-continue-opens-at-tokyo-disneyland|text=Star Tours: The Adventures Continue Opens at Tokyo Disneyland}} On [[November 16]], 2015, a new segment set on Jakku debuted as part of Disneyland's "Season of the Force" event in order to promote ''The Force Awakens''{{SW|url=news/8-things-to-look-for-at-disneylands-season-of-the-force|text=8 Things to Look for at Disneyland's Season of the Force}}, then premiered at Disney's Hollywood Studios on [[December 1]] of that year.{{DPB|url=2015/11/disneys-hollywood-studios-to-debut-new-star-wars-experiences-in-december|text=Disney's Hollywood Studios to Debut New Star Wars Experiences in December}} Additionally, variants of existing segments created for the original 2011 show were activated for the first time, such as a version of the Vader opening featuring Boba Fett, an altered Naboo segment where the StarSpeeder hits the Gungan while visiting Otoh Gunga and the windshield is splattered with paint at the hangar, and a version of the Coruscant ending where Chewbacca greets guests from the ''Millennium Falcon'' cockpit. On March 18, 2017, the Disneyland Paris version opened. While similar to its counterparts, Paris expanded on the "old droids in shipping containers" idea and relocated [[ROX-N]] and the original Goose Droids to additional shipping containers, with new builds of the upgraded GT droids taking over those spots.{{EndorExpress|url=star-wars-adventures/star-tours-2/2017/04/review-star-tours-laventure-continue|text=Review: Star Tours: l'Aventure Continue}} On April 15, 2017, it was announced at Star Wars Celebration Orlando that a new segment set on [[Crait]] would be added in order to promote ''The Last Jedi''.{{WebCite|url=https://screenrant.com/star-tours-last-jedi-location-crait|text=Star Tours Attraction Adding Star Wars: The Last Jedi Location|author=Welch, Alex|date=2017-04-15|archivedate=20190209144940}} The segment was activated on [[November 17]], 2017, and also included [[Batuu]], a planet created for [[Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge|''Star Wars'': Galaxy's Edge]].{{SW|url=news/welcome-to-batuu-star-wars-galaxys-edge-planet-revealed-on-star-tours|text=Welcome to Batuu: ''Star Wars'': Galaxy's Edge Planet Revealed on Star Tours}} The update also included a scene of [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]] and the [[First Order]], patterned after the similar Darth Vader scene that opened earlier ride variations. On [[October 23]], [[2019]], it was announced that [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''The Rise of Skywalker'']] content would be added on [[December 20]].{{SW|url=news/star-tours-star-wars-the-rise-of-skywalker-update|text=Star Tours to Receive ''Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker'' Update on December 20}} On March 5, 2024, it was announced that segments based around [[Star Wars: The Mandalorian|''The Mandalorian'']], ''[[Star Wars: Ahsoka|Ahsoka]]'', and ''[[Star Wars: Andor]]'' would be added to the attraction, and the new segments debuted on April 5. This brought the number of sequence combinations up to 250. ==Continuity== According to "[[The Incredible Journey]]," an article in [[Star Wars Insider 127|''Star Wars Insider'' 127]], the events of ''Star Tours: The Adventures Continue'' occurred at some point between ''Revenge of the Sith'' and ''A New Hope''.{{InsiderCite|127|The Incredible Journey}} While the attraction itself did not provide precise data about its chronology,''[[Star Tours: The Adventures Continue]]'' Admiral Ackbar's appearance set it after his rescue from Imperial slavery in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], and droid [[G2-9T/Legends|G2-9T's]] comment about the [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel headquarters]] also places it after that date.{{FactFile|121|VAS 1-2|The Yavin Vassilika}} The [[Star Wars Blog]] article ''[[Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours]]'' finally placed the ride's date at about 1 BBY. The seasonal ''Season of the Force'' version of the attraction, which included a scene set on Jakku, characters Finn and BB-8 and the First Order, was anachronistic with the timeline presented throughout the rest of the attraction. In one scene, riders were confronted by Darth Vader, and encountered Finn in the next. It can be assumed that the events presented in these scenes are non-canon. Further Sequel trilogy additions in 2017 smoothed out some of these issues by creating a new Kylo Ren intro and a reworked version of the scanning droid intro featuring First Order stormtroopers. The attraction is still randomized, but each flight includes either original content entirely based on Episodes I to VI, or entirely based on Episodes VII to IX. This avoids the most glaring continuity issues.{{EndorExpress|url=parks/disneyparks/tokyo-disneyland/2018/01/exclusive-no-more-time-travel-in-star-tours|text=Exclusive: No more time travel in Star Tours!}} ==Adaptations and tie-in media== [[File:Bree_rec.png|thumb|250px|[[Bree Starlighter]], a character used only in promotional material and mall tours prior to the opening of the new ride]] As with the original version, upon exiting the ride, passengers are led to a store which sells merchandise based on ''Star Wars'' and the ride itself. This includes action figures, clothing, etc. At Disneyland the store is [[The Star Trader]].{{Disney|subdomain=disneyland|domain=go.com|url=shops/disneyland/star-trader|text=Disneyland: The Star Trader}} At Disney's Hollywood Studios, the store is [[Tatooine Traders]], themed to resemble the buildings of Mos Eisley and Mos Espa.{{Disney|subdomain=disneyworld|domain=go.com|url=shops/hollywood-studios/tatooine-traders|text=Disney's Hollywood Studios: Tatooine Traders}} Both stores include some exclusive merchandise sold only at Disney theme parks, including action figures of the various droids seen in the ride and queue, and StarSpeeder 1000 toys.[[Star Wars: Star Tours (toy line)|''Star Wars: Star Tours'' toy line]] ==Easter eggs {{SectionFlag}}== ===In the queue=== The G2 repair droids in the queue line (also found in the original version of the queue) are actually the skeletons of [[Wikipedia:Audio-Animatronics|Audio-Animatronic]] geese from an old Disneyland show, [[Wikipedia:America Sings|''America Sings'']]. They were removed from the show during the last 2 years of its run. The droids can be heard singing variations of "I've been working on the railroad, all the livelong day," a reference to the song sung by the geese. ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' nicknamed the G2s "Goose droids" as a nod to this show.{{D23|url=9-out-of-this-world-facts-about-star-tours-30-years-of-continued-adventures|text=9 Out of This World Facts about Star Tours' Over 30 Years of Continued Adventures}} In the ride queue, after G2-9T somehow starts causing alarms to go off and gets a call from his supervisor, the droid at one point says "Uh, everything's perfectly alright now, w-we're fine. We're all fine here now, thank you. Uh, how are you?," a paraphrase of a line spoken by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in ''A New Hope''. He also says "Boring conversation, anyway" after cutting his supervisor off mid-sentence, which was also in the scene from the film.{{YouTube|video=io7HhGJfzRg|text=G2-9T Full Queue Loop - Part 1 (2012) Star Tours: The Adventures Continue|channel=channel/UCWmR60UYhQuUkhTF1BE1STQ|name=VFKAdventureRabbit}} One of the items scanned in the luggage by G2-9T was a [[Wikipedia:Goofy|Goofy]] doll, prompting him to say that the design was "really goofy." When finding a Goofy hat in another luggage set, G2-9T will mistake it for a [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] styled hat. A [[Wikipedia:Chip 'n' Dale|Chip and Dale]] doll set seen in scanned luggage has G2-9T calling them [[Cloning/Legends|clones]]. Upon discovering the [[Smoke generator lamp|magic lamp]] from [[Wikipedia:Aladdin (1992 Disney film)|''Aladdin'']], he mentions that the Star Tours' non-smoking policy extends to luggage as well. [[Wikipedia:WALL-E|WALL-E]], from the Pixar film of the same name, can also be seen in one of the scans, attempting to get G2-9T's attention. G2-9T fails to see this, and clears the suitcase without noticing. Other luggage cameos include [[Wikipedia:List of Haunted Mansion characters#Madame Leota|Madame Leota]] from the ''[[Wikipedia:The Haunted Mansion|Haunted Mansion]]'', a Stitch plush, Sorcerer Mickey's hat from Fantasia, Mike Wazowski's teddy bear from Monsters Inc., Indiana Jones's hat and whip, the Mighty Microscope and a snowflake from Adventure Thru Inner Space, and V.I.N.CENT from ''The Black Hole''. ===On the ride=== The idea of going to Kashyyyk was suggested by [[Wikipedia:John Lasseter|John Lasseter]], principal creative adviser at Walt Disney Imagineering, and chief creative officer at Disney and [[Wikipedia:Pixar|Pixar Animation Studios]].{{WebCite|url=http://travel.usatoday.com/alliance/destinations/mouseplanet/post/2012/05/Taking-a-Trip-on-Star-Tours/685697/1|text=Taking a Trip on Star Tours|work=USA Today|archivedate=20120507132108}} During the droid's scan in the stormtrooper opening, one of the images that flash by in the video hologram are photos of George Lucas.{{WebCite|url=https://allears.net/2011/09/12/d23-expo-2011-star-tours-the-adventures-continue|work=Allears.net|archivedate=20221031150345|text=D23 Expo 2011: Star Tours – The Adventures Continue}} In the scene on Coruscant, a video billboard can be seen advertising "[[Tomorrowland]]" in [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]].{{YouTube|video=l0Kcyg_m3F0|text=Star Tours: The Adventures Continue - The Ultimate Experience|channel=@WML95|name=WhereMagicLives95}} Disneyland's ''Star Tours'' attraction is located in that park's [[Wikipedia:Tomorrowland|Tomorrowland]] themed area. References to the original ride are included when Threepio echoes a line from RX-24, saying, "I've always wanted to do this" (referring to a pod race, instead of the original's Death Star trench run), and again when he yells, "Brakes! Brakes! Where are the brakes?!" while careening through the Naboo hangar. A third quotation was added in 2019 when the StarSpeeder arrives at Kef Bir, where Threepio comments, "R2, we've passed the Endor moon!" ==Media== ===Poster gallery=== File:StarTours_II_poster.jpg|Original poster File:Star_Tours_The_Rise_of_Skywalker_art.jpg|Advertisement for 2019 additions File:Star_Tours_2024_art.jpg|Advertisement for 2024 additions ==Credits== {{Credits |cast= *[[Anthony Daniels]] ....
